title: Hybrid Retrieval Prompt Orchestration Framework
tagline: Build an orchestration prompt chain that filters, reranks, and ground-answers search results.
scenario: A global fintech company is building a customer support assistant. The assistant retrieves data from a vector database of help documents. However, raw search results can contain irrelevant or outdated information, causing hallucinations. You need to build a comprehensive RAG prompting workflow that evaluates retrieved document relevance, reranks documents, runs compliance filters, and generates a grounded response.
brief: Create an advanced prompt system that orchestrates RAG context ingestion, document relevance filtering, cross-document consolidation, and final grounded answer generation with inline citations.
deliverables: A complete orchestration prompt template containing system instructions, context variables, formatting blocks, and guardrail rules.
A document-filtering validation instruction to classify retrieved documents as "relevant" or "irrelevant" based on the user question.
A detailed hallucination mitigation prompt strategy that forces the LLM to only answer using retrieved facts and cite sources.
An optimization analysis proposing token cost management, handling larger document payloads, and mitigating prompt drift.
submissionGuidance: Document the complete prompting architecture in Markdown. Provide detailed system instructions and explain the formatting variables and guardrails with code examples.

rubric.label: Hallucination Mitigation
rubric.description: Does the system prompt enforce strict grounding, and prevent answering when facts are missing?

rubric.label: Prompt Chain Orchestration
rubric.description: Is the sequence of filtering, citation-mapping, and answering logical and cohesive?

rubric.label: Token & Cost Management
rubric.description: Does the architecture present viable strategies for managing token cost and window limitations?
